get.description: Initiates the OIDC authorization flow by redirecting the user to the provider's login page.

302.description: Redirect to OAuth provider login page

get.description: Handles the callback from the OIDC provider, exchanges the authorization code for tokens, and redirects to the frontend.

302.description: Redirect to frontend with auth token in fragment

post.description: Creates a new account with email and password and returns an authentication token.
Authenticates with email and password and returns an authentication token.
Verifies a user's email address using the token from the verification email.
Resends the email verification link to the authenticated user.

get.description: Return the currently authenticated user. ``role`` and ``account`` are populated when the user has a membership; both are null when the caller is authenticated but has no account yet — e.g. a returning user who has just accepted an invitation email.

put.description: Update the currently authenticated user's profile.

get.description: Return the account for the currently authenticated user.

delete.description: Permanently delete the current account and all associated data.

post.description: Delete every config, flag, logger, log group, context, context type, environment, and customer API key (except the caller's current key) on the account. The `common` config is preserved as a structural anchor but its items are reset. Requires `OWNER` role and a body of `{"confirm": true}` — any other value returns 400. Pass `"generate_sample_data": true` to re-seed the account with the standard sample dataset after the wipe (best-effort; seeding failures are logged but do not fail the wipe). Returns 204 on success; 500 if any sub-delete fails.

get.description: Return the current user's settings as plain JSON.

put.description: Replace the current user's settings with the provided JSON object.
Set a single key in the current user's settings. The key is stored as a flat literal key (dot-notation is NOT expanded to nested paths). Returns the full updated settings object.

get.description: Return the current account's settings as plain JSON.

put.description: Replace the current account's settings with the provided JSON object. Requires admin role.

get.description: List all environments for the authenticated account.

post.description: Create a new environment. The caller provides the id (key) in the request body.

get.description: Return an environment by id.

delete.description: Delete an environment by id. When `cascade=true` is set, also remove every per-environment reference held by flags, configs, and loggers in the corresponding services before deleting the environment row. The default `cascade=false` deletes only the environment row, leaving downstream references in place.

parameters.name: cascade

schema.description: When `true`, remove every flag rule, env-level flag default, config override, and logger override scoped to this environment before deleting the environment row.
schema.title: Cascade

parameters.description: When `true`, remove every flag rule, env-level flag default, config override, and logger override scoped to this environment before deleting the environment row.

get.description: Report how many flag rules, env-level flag defaults, config overrides, and logger overrides reference this environment. Used by the console's delete dialog so the user can see what would survive a non-cascading delete.

post.description: Create a new API key. The id and key value are server-generated.

get.description: List all API keys for the authenticated account.
Return an API key by id.

put.description: Update an API key by id.

delete.description: Delete an API key by id.

post.description: Permanently revoke an API key.

get.description: List all context types for the authenticated account.

post.description: Create a new context type. The caller provides the id (key) in the request body.

get.description: Return a context type by id.

put.description: Update a context type by id.

delete.description: Delete a context type and all its associated context instances by id.

post.description: Register context instances in bulk. Creates context types automatically if they don't exist.

get.description: List all context instances for the authenticated account.
Return a context instance by composite id (type:key).

put.description: Update a context instance by composite id (type:key). Only the human-readable display name is mutable here; `context_type` and observed `attributes` are written by SDK registration and ignored on this endpoint.

delete.description: Delete a context instance by composite id (type:key).

get.description: List all services for the authenticated account.

post.description: Create a new service. The caller provides the id (key) in the request body.

get.description: Return a service by id.

post.description: Send one or more invitations to join the account.

get.description: List invitations. Authenticated admins list invitations for their own account and may narrow by status. Unauthenticated callers must pass ``filter[token]`` to look up a specific invitation by its token — used to render the invitation preview before sign-in. The token-filter path always returns an array of 0 or 1 elements.

post.description: Revoke a pending invitation by id.
Resend a pending invitation email by id.
Accept an invitation using a token from the invitation email.
Submit an email address to the smplkit launch list. Sends a notification to support@smplkit.com. No authentication required. Nothing is persisted.
Send a contact-us message. Delivers two emails: a ticket to support with Reply-To set to the user, and an auto-response to the user. Nothing is persisted; the returned id is for correlation only.

attributes.body: Hi, I have a question about the pro plan pricing...

get.description:
Return distinct metric names recorded for the account, each with a
representative unit. Plain-JSON response (not JSON:API) — this is
metadata for discovery, not a metric resource.

post.description: Ingest pre-aggregated metric data points. Returns 202 Accepted with no response body.

get.description: Query raw metric rows with filtering by name, time range, and dimensions.
Query aggregated metric rollups. Requires filter[rollup] for the aggregation interval.
List users in the authenticated account.

parameters.name: filter[search]

schema.description: Case-insensitive substring match against display_name and email. If the value is a valid UUID, also matches user id exactly.
schema.title: Filter[Search]

parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match against display_name and email. If the value is a valid UUID, also matches user id exactly.

get.description: Return a user by id.

put.description: Update a user's role in the account.

delete.description: Remove a user from the account.

get.description: Return all plan tier definitions as JSON:API resources.
Return all flag-enabled products with their plans, limits, and marketing content.
Return subscription rows for the authenticated account.

post.description: Create a new paid subscription for a product.
Upgrade an existing paid subscription to a higher plan.
Downgrade an existing paid subscription to a lower plan.
Cancel a subscription at end of the current billing period.
Reverse a pending cancellation; subscription will renew as normal.
Reverse a pending downgrade scheduled for end of the current billing period.

get.description: List all payment methods for the account. Default is returned first, then newest first.

post.description: Register a Stripe payment method (`pm_...`) on the account. The client first creates the Stripe payment method using a SetupIntent and Stripe Elements, then submits its identifier here to persist it.

get.description: Return a payment method by id.

put.description: Update the mutable fields of a payment method (`billing_details`, `exp_month`, `exp_year`). The `default` flag is not mutable via PUT — use the `set_default` action instead.

delete.description: Delete a payment method. Returns 409 if this is the only payment method on file and the account has an active paid subscription. If the deleted payment method was the default, the oldest remaining payment method is promoted to default.

post.description: Mark this payment method as the account's default. Idempotent: returns 200 with no changes when the payment method is already the default.

get.description: Return invoice history for the account from Stripe.

Return a single invoice by id. Supports content negotiation via the
`Accept` header:

- `application/pdf` — streams the invoice PDF.
- `application/vnd.api+json`, `application/json`, or absent — returns
  the JSON:API invoice resource.
- Any other value — `406 Not Acceptable`.

200.description: Successful response. Content type depends on the Accept header: application/vnd.api+json returns a JSON:API invoice resource; application/pdf streams the invoice PDF from Stripe.

406.description: Accept header specifies an unsupported media type

post.description:
Create a Stripe SetupIntent for adding a payment method without an
immediate charge. Returns the `client_secret` to pass to Stripe Elements
in the browser.

key.title: Key
key.description: Stable URL-safe identifier for the account, derived from the account name at creation. Used in console URLs and other places that prefer a human-readable handle.

has_stripe_customer.title: Has Stripe Customer
has_stripe_customer.description: `true` once the account has been linked to a billing provider customer record.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: When the account is scheduled to expire. `null` for accounts with no expiry.

deleted_at.title: Deleted At
deleted_at.description: When the account was deleted. `null` for active accounts.

product_subscriptions.title: Product Subscriptions
product_subscriptions.description: Map of product key to the account's subscription summary for that product, including plan, status, and entitlement limits.

entry_point.title: Entry Point
entry_point.description: How the account first reached smplkit (e.g. `LOGIN`, `GET_STARTED`, `LIVE_DEMO`).

show_sample_data.title: Show Sample Data
show_sample_data.description: Whether the account is currently configured to display the sample dataset alongside the customer's own resources.

discount_override_pct.title: Discount Override Pct
discount_override_pct.description: Custom discount percentage applied to the account in place of the volume-based discount schedule. `null` means the volume schedule applies.

discount_override_reason.title: Discount Override Reason
discount_override_reason.description: Free-form note explaining why the override was set.

discount_override_set_by_user_id.title: Discount Override Set By User Id
discount_override_set_by_user_id.description: UUID of the user who set the override.

discount_override_set_at.title: Discount Override Set At
discount_override_set_at.description: When the override was last changed.

Account.title: Account
Account.description:
A tenant of smplkit — the unit of isolation that owns all of a
customer's resources (environments, contexts, API keys, and so on).

AccountRequest.title: AccountRequest
AccountRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating or updating an account.

AccountResource.title: AccountResource
AccountResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for an account.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

AccountResponse.title: AccountResponse
AccountResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for an account.

confirm.title: Confirm
confirm.description: Must be `true` for the wipe to proceed. Any other value returns 400.

generate_sample_data.title: Generate Sample Data
generate_sample_data.description: When `true`, re-seed the account with the standard sample dataset after wiping. Best-effort: any seeding failure is logged but does not fail the wipe.

AccountWipeRequest.title: AccountWipeRequest
AccountWipeRequest.description: Confirmation envelope for the wipe-account action.

stripe_payment_method_id.title: Stripe Payment Method Id
stripe_payment_method_id.description: Identifier of the Stripe payment method to register on the account, e.g. `pm_1234567890abcdef`.

default.title: Default
default.description: When `true`, make the newly registered payment method the account's default. The first payment method on an account is always set as default regardless of this field.

AddPaymentMethodAttributes.title: AddPaymentMethodAttributes
AddPaymentMethodAttributes.description:
Attributes accepted when registering a new payment method.

The customer first creates a Stripe payment method client-side using
Stripe Elements, then submits its `pm_...` identifier here to persist
it on the account.

AddPaymentMethodBody.title: AddPaymentMethodBody
AddPaymentMethodBody.description: JSON:API request envelope for registering a new payment method.

AddPaymentMethodData.title: AddPaymentMethodData
AddPaymentMethodData.description: Resource object for the add-payment-method request.

status.title: Status
status.description: Lifecycle state of the key. `ACTIVE` keys may be used to authenticate; `REVOKED` keys are rejected.

key.title: Key
key.description: The bearer token value. Returned in plaintext on the create response so the caller can capture it; subsequent reads return the same value for round-tripping.

scopes.title: Scopes
scopes.description: Scope restrictions applied to the key. Empty object grants full account access; populated forms are reserved for future scope syntax.

created_by.title: Created By
created_by.description: UUID of the user who created the key.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: Optional expiry timestamp. After this time, the key is rejected. Omit for keys that do not expire.

last_used_at.title: Last Used At
last_used_at.description: When the key was most recently used to authenticate.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the key was last modified.

ApiKey.title: ApiKey
ApiKey.description:
An API key used by SDKs, scripts, and other programmatic clients to
authenticate with the smplkit API on behalf of the account.

The full key value is returned in plaintext on the create response and
is otherwise unavailable — record it somewhere safe immediately after
creation.

ApiKeyListResponse.title: ApiKeyListResponse
ApiKeyListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response for API keys.

ApiKeyRequest.title: ApiKeyRequest
ApiKeyRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating or updating an API key.

ApiKeyResource.title: ApiKeyResource
ApiKeyResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for an API key.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

ApiKeyResponse.title: ApiKeyResponse
ApiKeyResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for an API key.

token.title: Token
token.description: Bearer token to pass in the `Authorization` header.

AuthTokenResponse.title: AuthTokenResponse
AuthTokenResponse.description: Authentication token issued on successful login or registration.

ContactTopic.title: ContactTopic
ContactTopic.description: Topic options accepted on contact-us submissions.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable display name for the context instance.

context_type.title: Context Type
context_type.description: Key of the context type this instance belongs to (e.g. `user`, `account`).

attributes.title: Attributes
attributes.description: Observed attribute values for this context instance. The key set is conventionally aligned with the parent context type's known attribute keys, but additional keys are accepted.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: When the context instance was first registered.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the context instance was last modified.

Context.title: Context
Context.description:
A specific instance of a context type — for example, a particular
user, account, or device — together with the attributes observed on it.

Context instances are addressed by a composite identifier of the form
`context_type:key` (e.g. `user:alice-123`).

registered.title: Registered
registered.description: Number of context instances that were created or updated.

ContextBatchResponse.title: ContextBatchResponse
ContextBatchResponse.description: Summary returned by the bulk context registration endpoint.

type.title: Type
type.description: Key of the context type this instance belongs to (e.g. `user`, `account`, `device`).

key.title: Key
key.description: Entity identifier within the context type, e.g. `user-123`.

attributes.title: Attributes
attributes.description: Observed attribute values for this context instance.

ContextBulkItem.title: ContextBulkItem
ContextBulkItem.description: One context instance in a bulk registration payload.

ContextBulkRegister.title: ContextBulkRegister
ContextBulkRegister.description: Bulk registration request body for the contexts endpoint.

ContextListResponse.title: ContextListResponse
ContextListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response for context instances.

ContextRequest.title: ContextRequest
ContextRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating or updating a context instance.

ContextResource.title: ContextResource
ContextResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for a context instance.

`id` is the composite identifier `context_type:key`. It must not be
specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

ContextResponse.title: ContextResponse
ContextResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for a context instance.

name.title: Name
name.description: Display label for the context type, e.g. `User`, `Account`, or `Device`.

attributes.title: Attributes
attributes.description: Map of known attribute key to per-attribute metadata. The metadata object is free-form and may be empty. Keys grow as new attributes are observed on context instances of this type.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: When the context type was created.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the context type was last modified.

ContextType.title: ContextType
ContextType.description:
A kind of context — for example, `user`, `account`, or `device` — that
groups together context instances sharing a common set of attributes.

The known attribute keys for the type accumulate as instances are
registered; each key carries an optional metadata object describing it.

ContextTypeListResponse.title: ContextTypeListResponse
ContextTypeListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response for context types.

ContextTypeRequest.title: ContextTypeRequest
ContextTypeRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating or updating a context type.

ContextTypeResource.title: ContextTypeResource
ContextTypeResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for a context type.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

ContextTypeResponse.title: ContextTypeResponse
ContextTypeResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for a context type.

product.title: Product
product.description: Product key to subscribe to, e.g. `flags`.

plan.title: Plan
plan.description: Plan key to subscribe on, e.g. `pro`.

payment_method.title: Payment Method
payment_method.description: UUID of a payment method on file to bill against. If omitted, the account's default payment method is used.

CreateSubscriptionAttributes.title: CreateSubscriptionAttributes
CreateSubscriptionAttributes.description: Attributes accepted when creating a new subscription.

CreateSubscriptionBody.title: CreateSubscriptionBody
CreateSubscriptionBody.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating a subscription.

CreateSubscriptionData.title: CreateSubscriptionData
CreateSubscriptionData.description: Resource object for the create-subscription request.

topic.description: Categorization of the message used to route it to the right inbox.

body.title: Body
body.description: Free-form text of the message. Trimmed before validation.

sent_at.title: Sent At
sent_at.description: When the message was accepted by the server.

Email.title: Email
Email.description:
A contact-us submission. Sending the resource delivers a support
ticket and an auto-response email; nothing is persisted. The `id`
returned on the response is a per-request correlation identifier.

example.body: Hi, I have a question about the pro plan pricing...

EmailResource.title: EmailResource
EmailResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for a contact-us submission.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

attributes.body: Hi, I have a question about the pro plan pricing...

EmailResponse.title: EmailResponse
EmailResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for a contact-us message.

color.title: Color
color.description: Display color used by the console to badge the environment. Accepts any CSS color string.

classification.title: Classification
classification.description: `STANDARD` for environments the customer explicitly manages; `AD_HOC` for environments auto-created from SDK traffic. Case-insensitive on input.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the environment was last modified.

Environment.title: Environment
Environment.description:
A named deployment context — for example, `production`, `staging`, or
`development`. Resources scoped to an environment (such as config items
and feature flags) are evaluated against environment-specific values.

EnvironmentRequest.title: EnvironmentRequest
EnvironmentRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating or updating an environment.

EnvironmentResource.title: EnvironmentResource
EnvironmentResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for an environment.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

EnvironmentResponse.title: EnvironmentResponse
EnvironmentResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for an environment.

flag_rules.title: Flag Rules
flag_rules.description: Number of feature-flag targeting rules scoped to this environment. Each flag may contribute multiple rules.

flag_env_defaults.title: Flag Env Defaults
flag_env_defaults.description: Number of feature flags that declare an environment-level default value for this environment.

config_overrides.title: Config Overrides
config_overrides.description: Number of config-item overrides keyed to this environment, summed across all configs.

logger_overrides.title: Logger Overrides
logger_overrides.description: Number of loggers with an environment-level level override for this environment.

EnvironmentUsage.title: EnvironmentUsage
EnvironmentUsage.description:
Counts of references to an environment held by other resources.

Returned by `GET /environments/{id}/usage` so the console can warn
the user about per-environment configuration that would survive a
bare environment-row deletion. Each count is the number of distinct
referencing resources, not the number of rule entries within them.

EnvironmentUsageResource.title: EnvironmentUsageResource
EnvironmentUsageResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for an environment-usage report.

EnvironmentUsageResponse.title: EnvironmentUsageResponse
EnvironmentUsageResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for environment-usage counts.

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address the invitation was sent to.

role.title: Role
role.description: Role to assign on acceptance. One of `ADMIN`, `MEMBER`, or `VIEWER`.

status.title: Status
status.description: Lifecycle state of the invitation. One of `PENDING`, `ACCEPTED`, `REVOKED`, or `EXPIRED`.

invited_by.title: Invited By
invited_by.description: UUID of the user who sent the invitation.

account_name.title: Account Name
account_name.description: Name of the account the recipient is being invited to join.

inviter_display_name.title: Inviter Display Name
inviter_display_name.description: Display name of the user who sent the invitation.

token.title: Token
token.description: Single-use token that the recipient redeems to accept the invitation. Echoed on responses so the inviting client can construct the acceptance link.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: When the invitation token stops being redeemable.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the invitation record was last modified.

Invitation.title: Invitation
Invitation.description:
An invitation for a person to join an account.

Invitations carry a time-limited token; the recipient redeems the
token to become a member of the inviting account at the assigned role.

token.title: Token
token.description: Invitation token from the email link.

invitations.title: Invitations
invitations.description: One to fifty invitations to send in a single request.

InvitationBulkCreateRequest.title: InvitationBulkCreateRequest
InvitationBulkCreateRequest.description: Bulk-create request body for the invitations endpoint.

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address to send the invitation to.

role.title: Role
role.description: Role to assign on acceptance. One of `ADMIN`, `MEMBER`, or `VIEWER`. `OWNER` cannot be assigned via invitation. Case-insensitive on input.

InvitationCreateItem.title: InvitationCreateItem
InvitationCreateItem.description: One invitation in a bulk-create request.

InvitationResource.title: InvitationResource
InvitationResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for an invitation.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

InvitationResponse.title: InvitationResponse
InvitationResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for an invitation.

number.title: Number
number.description: Invoice number assigned by the billing provider.

amount_due.title: Amount Due
amount_due.description: Amount owed on the invoice in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents).

amount_paid.title: Amount Paid
amount_paid.description: Amount paid against the invoice in the smallest currency unit.

description.title: Description
description.description: Human-readable summary of the invoice's line items.

period_start.title: Period Start
period_start.description: Start of the service period the invoice covers (ISO 8601).

period_end.title: Period End
period_end.description: End of the service period the invoice covers (ISO 8601).

paid_at.title: Paid At
paid_at.description: When the invoice was paid in full (ISO 8601), or `null` if unpaid.

hosted_invoice_url.title: Hosted Invoice Url
hosted_invoice_url.description: Link to the hosted invoice page.

invoice_pdf.title: Invoice Pdf
invoice_pdf.description: Link to the PDF rendering of the invoice.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: A billing invoice issued for the account.

InvoiceResource.title: InvoiceResource
InvoiceResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for an invoice.

InvoiceSingleResponse.title: InvoiceSingleResponse
InvoiceSingleResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for an invoice.

description.title: Description
description.description: Long-form description of what the limit controls.

unit.title: Unit
unit.description: Unit the limit is measured in, e.g. `flags`, `events`.

display_format.title: Display Format
display_format.description: Optional formatter hint for rendering the limit value in customer-facing UI.

LimitDefinition.title: LimitDefinition
LimitDefinition.description: Description of a single metered limit on a product.

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address of the user signing in.

LoginRequest.title: LoginRequest
LoginRequest.description: Body for the email + password login endpoint.

value.title: Value
value.description: Aggregated value for this data point over `period_seconds`.

unit.title: Unit
unit.description: Unit the value is expressed in, e.g. `evaluations`, `ms`, `bytes`.

period_seconds.title: Period Seconds
period_seconds.description: Length of the aggregation window in seconds (e.g. `60` for a one-minute roll-up).

dimensions.title: Dimensions
dimensions.description: Optional dimension keys that scope the data point, e.g. `environment`, `service`. Used as filter targets on the list endpoint via `filter[dimensions.<key>]=...`.

recorded_at.title: Recorded At
recorded_at.description: Start of the aggregation window this data point covers.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: When the data point was ingested.

MetricAttributes.title: MetricAttributes
MetricAttributes.description: A pre-aggregated metric data point recorded for the account.

data.title: Data
data.description: Metric data points to ingest in a single request.

MetricBulkRequest.title: MetricBulkRequest
MetricBulkRequest.description: Bulk-ingest request envelope for metric data points.

MetricListResponse.title: MetricListResponse
MetricListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response for metric data points.

unit.title: Unit
unit.description: Representative unit observed for this series.

MetricNameItem.title: MetricNameItem
MetricNameItem.description: One distinct metric name with a representative unit.

MetricNamesResponse.title: MetricNamesResponse
MetricNamesResponse.description: Plain-JSON response listing distinct metric names for the account.

MetricResource.title: MetricResource
MetricResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for a metric data point.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

name.title: Name
name.description: Metric series name the rollup is computed from.

value.title: Value
value.description: Sum of the underlying metric values over the bucket.

unit.title: Unit
unit.description: Unit the value is expressed in.

bucket.title: Bucket
bucket.description: Start of the time bucket this rollup covers.

MetricRollupAttributes.title: MetricRollupAttributes
MetricRollupAttributes.description: An aggregated metric value over a fixed-size time bucket.

MetricRollupListResponse.title: MetricRollupListResponse
MetricRollupListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response for metric rollups.

MetricRollupResource.title: MetricRollupResource
MetricRollupResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for a metric rollup.

products_needed.title: Products Needed
products_needed.description: Number of additional subscribed products needed to reach the next tier.

discount_pct.title: Discount Pct
discount_pct.description: Discount percentage that would apply at the next tier.

additional_savings_cents.title: Additional Savings Cents
additional_savings_cents.description: Additional monthly savings in cents at the next tier.

NextTierMeta.title: NextTierMeta
NextTierMeta.description: Information about the next volume-discount tier.

size.title: Size
size.description: Page size used for this response.

total_items.title: Total Items
total_items.description: Total number of matching items across all pages.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the current page size.

PageMeta.title: PageMeta
PageMeta.description: Pagination metadata returned with a collection response.

last4.title: Last4
last4.description: Last four digits of the card number.

default.title: Default
default.description: Whether this payment method is the account's default for subscription charges. Use the `set_default` action to change which payment method is default — this field is not writable via PUT.

billing_details.title: Billing Details
billing_details.description: Billing details (name, email, phone, address) associated with the card.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: When the payment method was registered.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the payment method was last modified.

PaymentMethod.title: PaymentMethod
PaymentMethod.description:
A saved card on file for the account, used to charge subscription
invoices.

The default payment method is changed via the `set_default` action
rather than by updating this field through PUT.

PaymentMethodListResponse.title: PaymentMethodListResponse
PaymentMethodListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response for payment methods.

PaymentMethodRequest.title: PaymentMethodRequest
PaymentMethodRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for updating a payment method.

PaymentMethodResource.title: PaymentMethodResource
PaymentMethodResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for a payment method.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

PaymentMethodResponse.title: PaymentMethodResponse
PaymentMethodResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for a payment method.

sort_order.title: Sort Order
sort_order.description: Order in which the plan should be shown in customer-facing lists. Lower values sort first.

Plan.title: Plan
Plan.description: A plan tier offered across smplkit products.

plan.title: Plan
plan.description: Plan key to change the subscription to.

PlanChangeRequest.title: PlanChangeRequest
PlanChangeRequest.description: Body for the subscription upgrade and downgrade actions.

price_monthly_cents.title: Price Monthly Cents
price_monthly_cents.description: Monthly list price in cents. `0` for free plans.

limits.title: Limits
limits.description: Map of limit key to the cap that applies on this plan. `-1` indicates an unlimited cap.

PlanDefinition.title: PlanDefinition
PlanDefinition.description: Per-plan pricing and limits for a product.

PlanListResponse.title: PlanListResponse
PlanListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response for plan tiers.

PlanResource.title: PlanResource
PlanResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for a plan tier.

attributes.description: Get started with essential features at no cost

tagline.title: Tagline
tagline.description: Short marketing tagline shown on plan-selection surfaces.

features.title: Features
features.description: Bullet-list feature highlights for the product.

coming_soon.title: Coming Soon
coming_soon.description: When `true`, the product is listed but not yet available for subscription.

limits.title: Limits
limits.description: Map of limit key to limit definition for this product.

plans.title: Plans
plans.description: Map of plan key to plan definition for this product.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: A smplkit product, with its plans, metered limits, and marketing copy.

ProductResource.title: ProductResource
ProductResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for a product catalog entry.

attributes.tagline: Feature flag delivery for safer rollouts and controlled experiments.

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address that becomes the new user's login identifier.

password.title: Password
password.description: Password for the new account. Must be at least 8 characters.

entry_point.title: Entry Point
entry_point.description: How the customer arrived at the registration page. Allowed values: `LOGIN`, `GET_STARTED`, `LIVE_DEMO`, `UNKNOWN`. Defaults to `UNKNOWN` when omitted. Case-insensitive on input.

RegisterRequest.title: RegisterRequest
RegisterRequest.description: Body for the email + password registration endpoint.

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the service was last modified.

Service.title: Service
Service.description:
A service that contexts can be evaluated against — for example, a
backend application or microservice in the customer's stack.

ServiceRequest.title: ServiceRequest
ServiceRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating or updating a service.

ServiceResource.title: ServiceResource
ServiceResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for a service.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

ServiceResponse.title: ServiceResponse
ServiceResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for a service.

client_secret.title: Client Secret
client_secret.description: Client secret to pass to Stripe Elements so the customer can complete payment-method setup in the browser.

SetupIntentAttributes.title: SetupIntentAttributes
SetupIntentAttributes.description: Result of executing the setup-intent function.

SetupIntentResponse.title: SetupIntentResponse
SetupIntentResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for a setup-intent result.

product.title: Product
product.description: Product key the subscription is for, e.g. `flags`.

plan.title: Plan
plan.description: Plan key the subscription is on, e.g. `pro`.

status.title: Status
status.description: Lifecycle state of the subscription, e.g. `active`, `trialing`, `past_due`, `canceled`.

comped.title: Comped
comped.description: When `true`, the subscription is complimentary and is not billed through the billing provider.

stripe_managed.title: Stripe Managed
stripe_managed.description: When `true`, the subscription is billed through Stripe; otherwise it is a free or complimentary subscription that does not produce invoices.

current_period_end.title: Current Period End
current_period_end.description: End of the current billing period (ISO 8601 timestamp).

client_secret.title: Client Secret
client_secret.description: Stripe payment intent client secret returned when a subscription create requires additional authentication (3DS). Returned only on create.

SubscriptionAttributes.title: SubscriptionAttributes
SubscriptionAttributes.description: A subscription that grants the account access to a product on a plan.

subtotal_cents.title: Subtotal Cents
subtotal_cents.description: Sum of list prices across all subscriptions in cents.

discount_source.title: Discount Source
discount_source.description: Source of the discount. `VOLUME` indicates the standard volume-discount schedule; `OVERRIDE` indicates a custom discount set on the account.

total_cents.title: Total Cents
total_cents.description: Final monthly total in cents after the discount.

next_tier.description: Information about the next volume-discount tier, or `null` if the account is already at the top tier or on an override.

SubscriptionListMeta.title: SubscriptionListMeta
SubscriptionListMeta.description: Discount and totals summary attached to a subscription collection response.

SubscriptionResource.title: SubscriptionResource
SubscriptionResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for a subscription.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

SubscriptionResponse.title: SubscriptionResponse
SubscriptionResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for a subscription.

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address used to sign in to the user account.

display_name.title: Display Name
display_name.description: Human-readable display name shown in the console and on shared resources.

profile_pic.title: Profile Pic
profile_pic.description: URL of an external profile picture (e.g. the value supplied by the user's identity provider).

avatar_url.title: Avatar Url
avatar_url.description: Server-generated `data:` URL containing the user's avatar image bytes when one has been captured. `null` when no avatar is available — callers should fall back to Gravatar or initials.

auth_provider.title: Auth Provider
auth_provider.description: Identity provider that authenticates the user, e.g. `google`, `microsoft`, or `email`.

email_verified.title: Email Verified
email_verified.description: Whether the user has completed email verification.

role.title: Role
role.description: Role the user holds in the current account context. One of `OWNER`, `ADMIN`, `MEMBER`, or `VIEWER`.

account.title: Account
account.description: UUID of the account the user is acting within.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: When the user record was created.

User.title: User
User.description: A person with access to one or more accounts in smplkit.

UserRequest.title: UserRequest
UserRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating or updating a user.

UserResource.title: UserResource
UserResource.description:
JSON:API resource envelope for a user.

`id` must not be specified for create requests (the server assigns it).

UserResponse.title: UserResponse
UserResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response envelope for a user.

token.title: Token
token.description: Verification token previously delivered to the user's email.

HTTPBearer.description: JWT access token or API key (sk_api_*)
